reason: The Wave A1 Kotlin port bound every engine family (reducer, comparator, navigation, completion) to :engine and drained this list. Wave 15.1 rewrote the check contract to the attributed-majority vote flow (PROTOCOL.md sections 6, 10; DESIGN.md D32); the Kotlin port does not yet compute the vote state machine, so check is unbound and skipped-until-engine here, mirroring packages/engine/vectors.skip.json. The Android engine rebind lands with Wave 15.6, which drains it from this list. A non-foreign family otherwise reappears here only if its engine binding is intentionally removed, which the per-family guard in engine/src/test then flags. Mirrors apps/ios/vectors.skip.json and packages/engine/vectors.skip.json.

foreign.reason: A foreign family's consumer is never :engine (PROTOCOL.md §13). client-store's Android consumer is :store, whose suite executes every case (mirroring the iOS store); clue-runs' consumer is the clue-run parser and renderer in :ui. The runner discovers and shape-validates these vectors but never executes them; execution lives in the consumer's suite. Unlike `families`, a foreign family is never bound to the engine and never leaves this list, so the per-family rebind that drains `families` must not touch it. Mirrors apps/ios/vectors.skip.json.
